content.problemStatement:
Tsetso likes to play with arrays.

An Array A[1,2,‚Ä¶,N]A[1,2,\ldots,N]A[1,2,‚Ä¶,N] is said to be a Tsetso array if A[1]‚čÖA[N]=max(A1,A2,‚Ä¶,AN‚ąí1,AN)A[1] \cdot A[N] = max(A_1,A_2,\ldots,A_{N-1},A_N)A[1]‚čÖA[N]=max(A1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚ąí1‚Äč,AN‚Äč).

Bedo, his teammate, likes to solve problems so much, so Tsetso gave him an array AAA of NNN distinct\bf{distinct}distinct integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč and QQQ queries of the form [L,R][L, R][L,R] and asked him to count the number of Tsetso subarrays in the given range.

A subarray[L,R][L,R][L,R] is to be a Tsetso subarray if A[L]‚čÖA[R]=max(AL,AL+1,....,AR‚ąí1,AR)A[L] \cdot A[R] = max(A_L,A_{L+1},....,A_{R-1},A_R)A[L]‚čÖA[R]=max(AL‚Äč,AL+1‚Äč,....,AR‚ąí1‚Äč,AR‚Äč).

Bedo spent a lot of time trying to solve it, but he couldn't, can you?

Note\bf{Note}Note: A sequence aaa is a subarray of a sequence bbb if aaa can be obtained from bbb by deletion of several (possibly, zero or all) elements from the beginning and several (possibly, zero or all) elements from the end.


Input Format

First-line will contain TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of input contains two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ, the number of elements in the array, and the number of queries.
The second line contains NNN distinct\bf{distinct}distinct space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.
Then QQQ lines follow each line containing two space-separated integers L,RL, RL,R.


Output Format
For each query, output in a single line the number of Tsetso subarrays in the given range [L,R][L, R][L,R].
constraints: Sum of NNN over all testcases is atmost 10510^5105.
Sum of QQQ over all testcases is atmost 5‚čÖ1055 \cdot 10^55‚čÖ105.
